sections.instruction: Complete the sentences and notes below. For Questions 11-15, write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer. For Questions 16-20, write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
For Questions 21-24, choose TWO letters A-E. For Questions 25-30, write the correct letter A, B or C next to questions 25-30.

questions.content: Which TWO reasons do the students give for choosing the university they attend?
options: A the opportunities to study abroad
C the wide choice of courses offered
E the reputation of the teaching staff

questions.content: Which TWO study groups are both students going to join?

questions.content:
31. Students need {{BLANK}} and persistence to succeed in maths.
32. Students also have a need to understand the {{BLANK}} of the subject.
33. It is the teacher’s role to make maths accessible. Maths is in everything we do, from using a {{BLANK}} to decorating a room.
34. Teachers should avoid using techniques involving {{BLANK}} or repetition.
35. People have applied similar {{BLANK}} for many generations.
36. Basic maths skills help us stick to a {{BLANK}} or understand trends.
37. Maths is essential in progressing our careers, much like {{BLANK}} skills.
38. It is also useful in hobbies like music or sailing. Students find it hard to see how maths relates to life outside school: studying risk assessment or {{BLANK}} makes this easier. Maths also helps us to find solutions to difficult life problems.
39. The way to study maths is to start from a {{BLANK}} of simple ideas and build up to more complex ones.
40. What teachers can do to help students see the point of maths; ask them to imagine life without calendars, watches, money or {{BLANK}}
